title: refund — product analytics

events.name: Refund Requested
events.description: Every refund request. Essential consent — financial-integrity signals cannot be opted out. days_since_payment_bucket: 0-7 / 8-30 / 31-90 / 91+; amount_bucket: 0-999 / 1000-4999 / 5000-19999 / 20000+.

events.name: Refund Approval Required
events.description: Fires when a refund enters awaiting_approval state. trigger IN ('amount_threshold', 'staleness_threshold', 'both', 'always_approve_reason').

events.name: Refund Cancelled
events.description: state_at_cancellation IN ('awaiting_approval', 'approved') — cancel is only allowed pre-dispatch.

events.name: Refund Processing
events.description: Fires on transition to processing. Provider dispatched; async wait begins.

events.name: Refund Succeeded
events.description: P1 event — every fire is a financial reversal. Essential consent. hours_to_success_bucket: 0-1 / 1-24 / 24-72 / 72-168 (1 week) / 168+ (weeks). For card refunds via Stripe/Adyen, typical is 120-240h (5-10 days).

events.name: Refund Provider Error
events.description: P1 event — non-terminal error requiring reconciliation. error_category IN ('network_timeout', 'rate_limit', 'transient_5xx', 'authentication', 'unknown'). Powers provider-reliability dashboards.

events.name: Refund Clawback Referral
events.description: P1 event — every fire is a downstream reward reversal impacting referrer credits.

events.name: Refund Clawback Coupon
events.description: P1 event — every fire is a downstream coupon usage-count decrement.

events.name: Refund Reconciled
events.description: drift_detected=true fires a P2 signal. Powers reconciliation-loop-quality dashboards.

notes: Amount buckets are pre-computed bins (0-999 / 1000-4999 / 5000-19999 / 20000+). Never raw amounts — prevents competitor cross-inference of refund-worthy tenant revenue.

Every RefundSucceeded / RefundFailed / RefundProviderError / RefundClawbackReferral / RefundClawbackCoupon is `essential` consent tier — financial-integrity signals cannot be opted out. State cancel + reconcile events are `functional` consent (opt-outable).
Provider-quality dashboards read failure_reason_category + error_category for per-provider reliability trending.

Marketing events (ad-network conversion attribution) are NOT here — they live on the marketing lane at /modules/finance/blueprints/refund/marketing.json with the `advertising` consent tier. RefundSucceeded fires BOTH the analytics event (essential) + the marketing event (advertising).
